summary_one_line: An attribute name spelling exactly the six-byte literal 'xmlns\0' so the default-namespace gate at SAX2.c:1171 takes its true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML attribute whose split name resolves to the exact byte sequence name[0]='x', name[1]='m', name[2]='l', name[3]='n', name[4]='s', name[5]=0 (the literal 'xmlns' with no prefix and ns==NULL), satisfying the full six-character equality chain at SAX2.c:1170-1171.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'xmlns=' token in their bytes (the ASCII run 'xmlns' is the program-side target of the gate) and value_profile_cmplog can splice the exact 5-char/NUL-terminated comparand into the right input offset via I2SRandReplace; the winner reaches the false/resolving side at line 1160 and 1333 ([W] tags) and resolves 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile only has havoc plus a CMP_MAP distance gradient; for a 6-way single-byte equality chain that terminates on a NUL, the prefix-distance bucket gives no useful gradient past the matched prefix, so the loser blocks 8/10 trials and only reaches the gate's true side once.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: cmplog records the per-character comparand bytes ('x','m','l','n','s',0) at the SAX2.c:1170-1171 CMPs and substitutes them into the matching input offset, planting the exact 'xmlns\0' name that value_profile's Hamming/prefix gradient alone cannot synthesize.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is a six-byte exact-literal equality chain spelling 'xmlns' terminated by NUL.
The gate's first half checks name[0..2] equal 'x','m','l' with ns==NULL and non-HTML context.
The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating value_profile with an I2S technique delta.
The winner resolves 8/10 trials while the loser blocks 8 and resolves only 1.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'xmlns' / 'xml' ASCII run the gate compares against.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, only havoc plus token stack.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of the havoc and token stack.
value_profile only exposes a coarse prefix/Hamming distance bucket, not exact substitution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6193 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed f120aaa512a7ba5e` and observed 2 I2S-floor rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 8 and 9 of the ancestor chain, the dash-row floor signal characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same 6-byte 'xmlns\0' equality-chain gate where value_profile resolves it as fast as value_profile_cmplog, showing the CMP_MAP prefix gradient alone suffices and I2S substitution is not the discriminator.

weakest_evidence_point: The winner seeds show 'xml' / 'xmlns=' bytes but the exact name[]='xmlns\0' value reaching the gate depends on xmlSplitQName output, which the seed-byte view does not directly confirm.
